_meta.note: First real regression pins for the EDA notebook exemplar. Every value re-derived from the deterministic src/eda functions against the shipped data/measurements.csv, not transcribed from prose. Manuscript source: manuscript/03_results.md.

dataset_subject_record_count.reason: Raw subject-record count of the shipped deterministic CSV fixture; the '120 subject records' claim in manuscript/03_results.md.
dataset_subject_record_count.script: cd projects/templates/template_eda_notebook && uv run --no-sync python -c 'from src.eda.dataset import load_dataset; print(len(load_dataset()))'  # -> 120

missingness_rows_dropped.reason: Rows removed by clean_dataset() for missing numeric features; the 'four rows are removed' claim in the Dataset and missingness section.
missingness_rows_dropped.script: cd projects/templates/template_eda_notebook && uv run --no-sync python -c 'from src.eda.dataset import load_dataset; from src.eda.cleaning import clean_dataset; _,r=clean_dataset(load_dataset()); print(r.dropped)'  # -> 4

strongest_pair_height_weight_value.reason: Pearson correlation of the dominant feature pair (height ~ weight), the top-ranked distinct pair from strongest_pairs on complete-case data; backs the 'strongly positively correlated' / 'dominant relationship is height ~ weight' claim.
strongest_pair_height_weight_value.script: cd projects/templates/template_eda_notebook && uv run --no-sync python -c 'from src.eda.dataset import load_dataset; from src.eda.cleaning import clean_dataset; from src.eda.correlation import correlation_matrix, strongest_pairs; c,_=clean_dataset(load_dataset()); print(strongest_pairs(correlation_matrix(c))[0])'  # -> ('height_cm','weight_kg', 0.7200855106986739)

second_pair_height_hr_value.reason: Pearson correlation of the second-ranked pair (height ~ resting heart rate); backs the 'comparatively weak height ~ resting heart rate (slightly negative)' claim. Sign matters: the pin is negative.
second_pair_height_hr_value.script: cd projects/templates/template_eda_notebook && uv run --no-sync python -c 'from src.eda.dataset import load_dataset; from src.eda.cleaning import clean_dataset; from src.eda.correlation import correlation_matrix, strongest_pairs; c,_=clean_dataset(load_dataset()); print(strongest_pairs(correlation_matrix(c))[1])'  # -> ('height_cm','resting_hr_bpm', -0.12098307049852419)

height_histogram_counts_sum.reason: Sum of the 10 height histogram bin counts; the figure caption asserts 'the bin counts sum to the number of complete-case rows'. Equals rows_out = 116.
height_histogram_counts_sum.script: cd projects/templates/template_eda_notebook && uv run --no-sync python -c 'from src.eda.dataset import load_dataset; from src.eda.cleaning import clean_dataset; from src.eda.figures import histogram_data; c,_=clean_dataset(load_dataset()); print(sum(histogram_data(c,"height_cm",bins=10).counts))'  # -> 116

dataset_subject_record_count.claim_text: The dataset contains 120 subject records across three groups (alpha, beta, gamma) with three numeric features.

missingness_rows_dropped.claim_text: clean_dataset() then drops rows missing any numeric feature ... With the shipped data, four rows are removed, leaving a complete-case dataset.

strongest_pair_height_weight_value.claim_text: the dominant relationship is height ~ weight (a strong positive correlation, by design) ... Height and weight are strongly positively correlated.

second_pair_height_hr_value.claim_text: followed by the comparatively weak height ~ resting heart rate (slightly negative) ... resting heart rate is only weakly related to the other two.

height_histogram_counts_sum.claim_text: The bin counts sum to the number of complete-case rows.
